instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-length` that evaluates a sequence and an expected length once each, signals an error if the lengths differ, and returns the sequence otherwise.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 PAIR) (#:G2 2))
            (UNLESS (= (LENGTH #:G1) #:G2)
              (ERROR "Expected length ~S, got ~S" #:G2 (LENGTH #:G1)))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 PAIR) (#:G113 2))
            (UNLESS (= (LENGTH #:G112) #:G113)
              (ERROR "Expected length ~S, got ~S" #:G113 (LENGTH #:G112)))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
